description:
Avoiding Mistakes: What to Do if You Forgot to Declare Pre-existing diseases in Health Insurance
In this video, we’ll cover an important issue many policyholders face—what happens if you forgot to declare a pre-existing disease when purchasing health insurance. We’ll explain what pre-existing diseases are, why insurance companies require them to be declared, and the potential consequences of not doing so. 

We’ll also guide you through the steps you can take to resolve this issue, including:
- Notifying your insurer about the undisclosed condition.
- Understanding policy terms like waiting periods and exclusions.
- Exploring corrective measures like policy endorsement or purchasing a new policy.
- The impact on future claims and how to avoid claim rejections.
 
This video is a must-watch for anyone with health insurance, as it provides practical advice to avoid complications in the future. Stay informed and protect your coverage.
customercare@insuranceseva.com
Toll free no- 18008900455
tags: best health insurance company in india

comments.text: I took a Tata AIG health insurance policy in 2020, I had no diseases at that time, when I was first admitted to the hospital in 2022, I was diagnosed with acute pancreatitis. At that time my claim was rejected. The reason given was pre-existing disease waiting period.  After that I was admitted to the same hospital again in September 2022. At that time, however, I first got approved by the policy company, then asked them to start my treatment, and my claim was accepted. In 2025, I was admitted to the same hospital again for the same reason and received appropriate treatment and the claim was accepted. When the renewal date approached, I told the policy company to mention my illness in my policy at this time. When I informed them via email, they canceled the previous policy. So what should I do now?
comments.author: @nil2488

comments.text: Sir, i have taken insurance in 2022 and its almost 2.5 years and i have diagnosed with Ulcerative colitis two months back.  Now  its my policy renewal time so should i mentioned ulcerative colotis??

comments.text:
Sir need your guidance, I have taken Max Bupa/Niva Bupa insurance since 2017 and I got AVN diagnosed in 2018 which I never declared. As Pre Existing Waiting period was 2 years and that clause has already passed away as today is 2025 the policy is in continuation till now. I have never taken any claim from Niva Bupa till date. Please guide me for two cases 
1. Is there any chances of Rejecting Claims by Niva Bupa in future for this Pre-existing disease?
2. Second query is if I port out my Policy to New India Assurance company, or any other. Does the Pre-existing disease waiting period again activate or covered. Also do I need to disclose the AVN as PED when Porting Out the Policy.? Please reply Sir.
comments.author: @riteishpanchal141

comments.text:
Hi Sir,
I have already completed my 15+ yrs of health insurance without any claim (with HDFC Ergo 10+ and Care 4+). Now I have ported the policy from care to ICICI Lombard. After disclosing every thing and providing all the documents over the phone and email, doctor verification done,  ICICI Lombard verified my details and confirmed the that I will be covered from Day 1 including PED and all other critical illnesses with ported sum assured of Rs. 980000/-.

I have made the payment and they issued the policy with the waiting period of 3 years and also reduced the sum assured. Now no-one is listening me, I am mentally to much harassed, 

All I want is my modified policy as ICICI Lombard confirmed and promised. What can I do?

comments.text:
Dear Viewer,
Once a health insurance policy is issued, any disease diagnosed afterward will not affect the terms and conditions of the policy. The coverage, benefits, and exclusions will remain the same as per the original policy terms.  

For any clarifications, please refer to the policy document or contact our support team.
